expectations.text: A0: Agent produced both output files with real content
expectations.evidence: analysis.md (91 lines) and architecture.md (59 lines) both contain substantive content
Selected: Microservices with 15/15 fit score. Service-Based acknowledged as runner-up.

expectations.text: A2: Ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles
expectations.evidence: Table in Step 2 ranks all 8: Microservices, Service-Based, Event-Driven, Space-Based, Microkernel, Service-Oriented, Modular Monolith, Layered

expectations.text: A3: Fitness function table with style-specific structural FFs (not just characteristic FFs)
expectations.evidence: 5 structural FFs: No shared database, Independent deployability, API contract compliance, No shared libraries with business logic, Service size bounds. All are structural invariants of microservices.

expectations.text: A4: Style fitness functions are specific to the selected style (microservices)
expectations.evidence: FFs enforce microservices-specific invariants: no shared DB (microservices data isolation), independent deployability, API contract compliance (inter-service communication), no shared business logic libs. These would not apply to a modular monolith.

expectations.text: A5: Fitness functions include Tool/Approach column
expectations.evidence: Tool/Approach column present: DB connection config analysis, Build pipeline check, Contract testing (Pact, Spring Cloud Contract), Dependency analysis on shared packages, Code metrics

expectations.text: A6: N/A - not an evolution path scenario
expectations.evidence: Eval 1 is not an evolution path scenario (evals 4 and 5 only)
